cases.passage: The Indian government has decided to impose a 10% import duty on crude oil to reduce the trade deficit. This move is expected to increase the domestic price of crude oil, which is a key input for the production of petrol and diesel. The petrol pump owners, who are the suppliers of petrol and diesel, will have to absorb the increased cost or pass it on to the consumers. Suppose the demand for petrol and diesel is inelastic, meaning that consumers will continue to buy the same quantity regardless of the price change. If the petrol pump owners pass on the increased cost to the consumers, what will happen to the quantity supplied of petrol and diesel in the market?

questions.q: What will happen to the quantity supplied of petrol and diesel if the petrol pump owners pass on the increased cost to the consumers?
opts: (C) Quantity supplied will remain the same
questions.explanation: When the price of petrol and diesel increases due to the import duty, the quantity supplied will decrease as the petrol pump owners reduce their production to minimize losses.

questions.q: Why is the demand for petrol and diesel considered inelastic in this scenario?
opts: (A) Because consumers have a low income
(B) Because consumers have a high income
(D) Because consumers have a medium income
questions.explanation: Inelastic demand means that consumers are not sensitive to price changes, so they will continue to buy the same quantity of petrol and diesel regardless of the price increase.

questions.q: What will happen to the quantity supplied of petrol and diesel if the petrol pump owners absorb the increased cost?
opts: (C) Quantity supplied will remain the same
questions.explanation: When the petrol pump owners absorb the increased cost, the price of petrol and diesel will remain the same, so the quantity supplied will not change.

questions.q: Why do the petrol pump owners pass on the increased cost to the consumers?
questions.explanation: The petrol pump owners pass on the increased cost to the consumers to minimize their losses, as they cannot absorb the increased cost and maintain their production levels.

questions.q: What is the effect of imposing a 10% import duty on crude oil on the quantity supplied of petrol and diesel?
opts: (C) Quantity supplied will remain the same
questions.explanation: The imposition of a 10% import duty on crude oil increases the domestic price of petrol and diesel, leading to a decrease in the quantity supplied.

cases.passage: The Indian government has decided to provide a subsidy to farmers to reduce the cost of production of wheat. This subsidy will reduce the price of wheat in the market, making it more attractive for consumers to buy. Suppose the demand for wheat is elastic, meaning that consumers will reduce their purchases in response to the price change. If the farmers are price takers, meaning that they have no control over the price of wheat, what will happen to the quantity supplied of wheat in the market?

questions.q: What will happen to the quantity supplied of wheat if the farmers are price takers?
opts: (C) Quantity supplied will remain the same
questions.explanation: When the price of wheat decreases due to the government subsidy, the quantity supplied will decrease as farmers reduce their production to maximize their profits.

questions.q: Why is the demand for wheat considered elastic in this scenario?
opts: (A) Because consumers have a low income
(B) Because consumers have a high income
(D) Because consumers have a medium income
questions.explanation: Elastic demand means that consumers are sensitive to price changes, so they will reduce their purchases in response to the price decrease.

questions.q: What will happen to the quantity supplied of wheat if the farmers can control the price of wheat?
opts: (C) Quantity supplied will remain the same
questions.explanation: When farmers can control the price of wheat, they will increase the quantity supplied to maximize their profits, as the price is now higher due to the subsidy.

questions.q: Why does the government provide a subsidy to farmers to reduce the cost of production of wheat?
opts: (A) To increase the profits of farmers
(B) To reduce the cost of production of wheat
(C) To increase the quantity supplied of wheat
questions.explanation: The government provides a subsidy to farmers to reduce the cost of production of wheat, making it more attractive for consumers to buy and increasing the quantity supplied in the market.

questions.q: What is the effect of providing a subsidy to farmers on the quantity supplied of wheat?
opts: (C) Quantity supplied will remain the same
questions.explanation: The subsidy reduces the price of wheat, making it more attractive for consumers to buy, and increasing the quantity supplied in the market.
